description:
Batch 4/5 — Lifecycle Management (1 of 2)

Wire the AgentPool drain capability into daemon stop and pause:

- ao daemon stop: set pool to drain mode (no new spawns), wait up to --shutdown-timeout-secs for running agents to complete, then force-cancel remaining, exit. Uses pool.drain() from TASK-109.
- ao daemon pause: set pool to drain mode, checkpoint running workflow states, stop housekeeping timer. Resume re-enables spawning and housekeeping.
- SIGTERM handler: trigger graceful drain (same as stop with default timeout)
- SIGINT (ctrl_c): already handled in coordinator loop, wire to drain

This replaces the current abrupt process kill on daemon stop. Ties into TASK-106 (graceful shutdown) but specifically focuses on the pool drain mechanics.
title: Add graceful pool drain to daemon stop and pause commands
